origin.name: Slovenia
origin.key_document: Smrtni list (death certificate from Maticni urad, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Slovenia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Ljubljana guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Slovenia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the Smrtni list, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Smrtni list (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Slovenia is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered with the Maticni urad (Registry Office) promptly.
All Slovenian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Slovenian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Ljubljana can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Slovenia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Slovenia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Registry Office, apostille, and Slovenian translation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
